entities.name: CHILD UNDERNUTRITION
entities.description: A state of insufficient nutrient intake in children leading to poor health outcomes

entities.name: DIETARY DIVERSIFICATION
entities.description: The practice of consuming a wide variety of foods to ensure adequate nutrient intake

entities.name: FOOD SAFETY
entities.description: Practices and conditions that ensure food is safe for consumption and free from contaminants

entities.name: HYGIENE PRACTICES
entities.description: Behaviors such as handwashing and sanitation to prevent disease and improve health

entities.name: KONGWA DISTRICT
entities.description: A rural district in central Tanzania where the study was conducted

entities.name: CENTRAL TANZANIA
entities.description: The region in Tanzania where the study took place

entities.name: DIFFERENCE-IN-DIFFERENCE
entities.description: A statistical technique used to estimate the causal effect of a specific intervention

entities.name: AFLATOXIN
entities.description: Toxic metabolites produced by certain molds that contaminate food and affect health

entities.name: ANTHROPOMETRIC MEASUREMENTS
entities.description: The measurement of physical characteristics of the human body to assess nutritional status

entities.name: WASTING
entities.description: A form of malnutrition characterized by low weight-for-height

entities.name: STUNTING
entities.description: A form of malnutrition characterized by low height-for-age

entities.name: UNDERWEIGHT
entities.description: A form of malnutrition characterized by low weight-for-age

entities.name: AFLATOXIN M1
entities.description: A metabolite of aflatoxin found in urine used as a biomarker for exposure

entities.name: Z-SCORES
entities.description: Standard deviations from the median of a reference population used to classify nutritional status

entities.name: INTEGRATED TECHNOLOGIES
entities.description: A combination of dietary, safety, and hygiene interventions used in training

entities.name: CHILDREN
entities.description: Human offspring under 24 months of age who were the subjects of the study

entities.name: MOTHERS
entities.description: Women who received training to improve the nutritional outcomes of their children
